description: Authoritative state of known ME/CFS drug trials in Germany. Updated by the routine in ROUTINE.md.

trials.name: Observational Study on Immunoadsorption (IA) in Patients With Autoantibody-Positive Post-Infectious ME/CFS

intervention.name: Immunoadsorption with TheraSorb® column
intervention.mechanism: Extracorporeal removal of autoantibodies (β2-adrenergic, GPCR) from blood plasma using TheraSorb® Ig column (Miltenyi); established treatment for autoimmune diseases

eligibility.summary: Adults 18–65 with ME/CFS per Canadian Consensus Criteria, Bell Score <60, detectable autoantibodies, who are already receiving immunoadsorption as clinical care
key_inclusion: Detectable autoantibodies (β2-adrenergic receptor or GPCR autoantibodies)

trials.notes: Immunoadsorption is a medical device/apheresis procedure (borderline per tracking rules — included and noted). Non-interventional observational study documenting 12-month outcomes in ME/CFS patients already receiving IA as standard care. Actual start date March 11, 2026; estimated completion December 31, 2027; 50 participants planned. PI: Prof. Dr. Carmen Scheibenbogen, Charité Berlin.

trials.name: Inebilizumab (Anti-CD19 Monoclonal Antibody) in Autoantibody-Mediated ME/CFS and Post-COVID Syndrome (Planned Pilot)

intervention.name: Inebilizumab (anti-CD19 monoclonal antibody)
intervention.mechanism: Monoclonal antibody targeting CD19 on B cells and plasmablasts; depletes autoantibody-producing cells; currently approved for neuromyelitis optica spectrum disorder

trials.condition: ME/CFS and post-COVID syndrome with autoantibody-mediated disease

eligibility.summary: Planned to enroll ~15 ME/CFS and post-COVID syndrome patients with confirmed autoantibody-mediated disease who previously responded to immunoadsorption
key_inclusion: ME/CFS or post-COVID syndrome with autoantibody evidence
Prior positive response to immunoadsorption (IA responders only)

trials.notes: Announced by Scheibenbogen at the 4th International ME/CFS Conference, Berlin, May 2026. No trial registry ID yet. Federal funding secured (reported cost ~€165,000/cycle). Study to select only immunoadsorption responders to enrich for autoantibody-mediated subgroup. Watch for DRKS/NCT registration.

trials.name: MDC002 — Phase 1 Clinical Trial in ME/CFS (Planned)

intervention.name: MDC002
intervention.mechanism: Orally applicable small molecule stimulating Na⁺/K⁺-ATPase and mitochondrial Na⁺/Ca²⁺ exchanger (NCLX) in skeletal muscle; targets post-exertional malaise and exertional intolerance by restoring muscle cell ion homeostasis

trials.condition: ME/CFS; post-acute infection syndromes with post-exertional malaise (PEM)

eligibility.summary: Trial design not yet announced. Target indication is ME/CFS and post-acute infection syndromes with post-exertional malaise.

trials.notes: Not yet registered in any trial registry. Mitodicure's website states MDC002 is 'advancing towards clinical trials' and described as ready for GLP toxicity and safety pharmacology studies as of 2026. Klaus Wirth is CSO and Managing Director of Mitodicure GmbH (German company, founded 2022). No Phase 1 sites confirmed yet; German origin makes a German site likely. Check mitodicure.com and registries each run for new registration.
